question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency polling interval to cause a connection pool starvation during a network partition: each poll attempt acquires a connection from the pool but the partition prevents the connection from being released, exhausting the pool and blocking all subsequent operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the setEnabled() call to be applied to only a subset of parser instances in a clustered deployment, creating an inconsistent enabled state where some parsers process files and others do not, leading to duplicate or missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the processingTimeout to cause a liveness probe failure loop: the timeout expires, the liveness probe fails, the container is restarted, the restart takes longer than the timeout, causing another liveness failure, creating an infinite restart loop that permanently takes the node offline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch window (5 minutes) to cause a consensus timestamp ordering violation: during a network partition, files from different time windows are batched together, and the parser processes them in the wrong order, violating the monotonically increasing consensus timestamp invariant?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency setting to cause a thread pool exhaustion during a network partition: each poll attempt spawns a new thread that blocks waiting for the partition to heal, and the thread pool fills up, preventing any new tasks from being scheduled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the isEnabled() check to be evaluated against a stale Spring Environment, where a recently disabled parser continues to process files because the configuration update was lost during the partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxFiles (1) limit during a network partition recovery to cause a sequential processing starvation: with only one file processed per batch cycle, a large backlog of files accumulated during the partition takes an unbounded amount of time to process, causing indefinite data staleness?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the processingTimeout to cause a false recovery signal: after a network partition heals, the processingTimeout window resets, making the health check report UP even though the parser has not yet caught up with the backlog of missed stream files, misleading consumers about data completeness?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch queueCapacity (10) to cause a backpressure failure during a network partition: the queue fills up, backpressure is applied to the downloader, the downloader stops downloading files, and when the partition heals the downloader and parser are both in a stalled state with no automatic recovery?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency polling loop to cause a metric counter overflow during a prolonged network partition: the parser increments a failure counter on every poll attempt at 20ms intervals, and after approximately 2^31 / 50 = ~12 hours the counter overflows, causing incorrect metric reporting?
